title: Cross-asset pooling of the meta-learner to relieve fold-starvation (events > features)
why: The BINDING constraint is fold-starvation (folds <30 trades -> should_stack rarely fires, DSR often NaN), which is an EVENTS/sample-size problem, not a feature problem. Bailey-LdP (DSR/MinBTL) + bias-variance literature: under a binding sample constraint, adding features past the first cheap one (B0134) is DOMINATED by adding OBSERVATIONS. The most literature-defensible event multiplier is pooling the meta-learner ACROSS ASSETS: turn N starved single-asset folds into one larger pooled training set, so the secondary ML model learns the act/pass decision from many more events while primaries stay per-asset. Directly attacks both the stack-gate starvation and the NaN-DSR symptom.
value_rationale: H: the expert-identified highest-EV lever after the one cheap feature (B0134). Relieves the binding constraint; unlocks the stack gate and real DSR verdicts.
scope: Pool meta-training events across assets (the H4 multi-asset roster) into one secondary-model training set; keep per-asset rule-based primaries and per-asset purge/embargo
Preserve the lookahead firewall + label-end purge PER ASSET within the pooled set (no cross-asset leakage; align by event time)
Add an asset-id / asset-features dimension so the pooled meta can condition on asset where signal differs
Re-evaluate should_stack + DSR on the pooled events (expect the gate to actually fire with adequate trade counts)
Mind: pooling assumes some transportability of the act/pass boundary across assets; validate with per-asset OOS, not just pooled
recommendation: Highest-value structural fix for fold-starvation; sequence AFTER the cheap B0134 win. Pairs with B0123 CPCV (more paths) once events are adequate.
out_of_scope: Pooling the PRIMARIES (they stay rule-based per asset). Changing the meta-label definition.
